=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}} == See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]]

revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], [[Category:pp error]]approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}} == See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]][[sn:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]] <!--Late Kofun tumuli--> [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:11]][[Category:End of the Kofun period]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Late Kofun tumuli]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]]

Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].

The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.

* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters

; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} - Diameter: 66 meters

revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|12=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}} == See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=draft}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)

Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers

This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].

The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.

Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters

; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters

; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|12=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=draft}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.

This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. ; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|12=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=draft}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.

Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers

Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.

This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|12=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=draft}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.

Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers

Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.

'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|12=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters

; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers

'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].

Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|12=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|春日向山古墳|" to "|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|"
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|12=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].

The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.

Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers

Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:qq]]" to ""
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|12=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=draft}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].

* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters

; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|大佐山白塚古墳|" to "|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|"
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|12=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=draft}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.

=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} * Charcoal chambers

Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
