revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to ""
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].

The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.

Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

revision.comment: 25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:終末期古墳 up to rev 99737344
revision.text:
'''終末期古墳'''（しゅうまつきこふん）は、[[古墳時代]]末から[[飛鳥時代]]にかけての[[7世紀]]頃の[[古墳]]を指す。[[森浩一]]によって最初に提唱された古墳の区分であり<ref>[[森浩一]]編『論集終末期古墳』1972年</ref>、この時代を古墳時代に含むとする考え方と含まないとする考え方がある。終末期古墳としては、[[千葉県]][[栄町]]の[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]や[[山武市]]の[[駄ノ塚古墳]]、[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[キトラ古墳]]や[[高松塚古墳]]などが、特に有名である。
== 概要 == [[6世紀]]末には[[前方後円墳]]築造の時代が終焉を迎えるという大きな変化があり<ref>[[広瀬和雄]]編『前方後円墳の終焉』2010年</ref>、それ以降[[律令制#日本の律令制|律令制]]に向かう段階で造営されたのが終末期古墳である。終末期古墳と認識されるようになるまでは、終末古墳、晩期古墳、飛鳥時代古墳などと呼ばれ、7世紀代の古墳の中でも特殊なものと捉えられていたが、高松塚古墳の調査を契機として終末期古墳として認識され始めた<ref>[[#河上|河上（2005年）6ページ]]</ref><ref>[[#斉藤|斉藤（2004年）]]</ref>。
=== 墳形=== 墳形には、[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]・[[八角墳]]・[[上円下方墳]]などがある。[[天皇陵]]に限っては、前方後円形から大型方墳に変わり、次に八角墳が採用されることになる。そして八角墳には、すべて八角形のものと上八角下方墳の2つのタイプがある。
=== 埋葬施設・副葬品 === 埋葬施設には、[[横穴式石室]]や[[横穴]]のほかに、各種の[[横口式石槨]]、木炭槨などがあり、[[棺]]には[[木棺]]・[[石棺]]以外に[[乾漆棺]]や[[須恵器]]の四注式屋根形[[陶棺]]などがある<ref>[[#永原|永原（1999年）818ページ]]</ref>。副葬品は概して少ない。銅貨銭が特色の一つである。
== 代表的な終末期の古墳 == * 最後の前方後円墳 : [[浅間山古墳 (栄町)|浅間山古墳]] - 墳丘全長78メートルの前方後円墳 : [[胡麻手台16号墳]] - 墳丘全長86メートルの前方後円墳 * 大型方墳 : [[龍角寺岩屋古墳]] - 一辺78メートル、終末期最大の方墳 : [[駄ノ塚古墳]] - 一辺62メートル、西暦[[610年]]から[[620年]]の間に造営された可能性が高い * 天皇陵 : [[春日向山古墳]]（[[用明天皇]]陵）- 63×60メートルの方墳 : [[山田高塚古墳]]（[[推古天皇]]陵）- 63×56メートルの方墳
=== 各地の終末期古墳 === ; 飛鳥地域 * [[赤坂天王山古墳]] - 一辺45メートルの方墳、巨大な横穴式石室 * [[段ノ塚古墳]]（[[舒明天皇|舒明]]陵） - 対角長約45メートルの八角形の墳丘を上に、その下は三段で最下段の幅約90メートル * [[秋殿南古墳]] - 一辺21メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[艸墓古墳]] - 一辺30メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[谷首古墳]] - 一辺35メートルの方墳、巨石の横穴式石室 * [[石舞台古墳]] - 一辺50メートルの周濠・堤、全面に貼石、巨大な横穴式石室 * [[都塚古墳]] - 一辺40メートルの方墳、横穴式石室<ref>{{Cite news |title=都塚古墳は巨大方墳 天皇陵に匹敵 |newspaper=共同通信 |date= |author= |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tLPrEEbKa6A |accessdate=2014-01-08}}</ref> * [[野口王墓|天武・持統陵]] - 対角長約45メートルの八角墳 * [[カナヅカ古墳|平田岩屋古墳]] - 一辺50メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[菖蒲池古墳]] - 方墳、横穴式石室は壁面全体に漆喰塗り、その中に特殊な[[家形石棺]]が2基 * [[岩屋山古墳]] - 一辺45メートルの方形の上に八角形（?）の墳丘、巨大な横穴式石室 * [[牽牛子塚古墳]] - 八角墳、斉明稜の可能性大、凝灰岩をくり抜いた横口式石槨 * [[高松塚古墳]] - 径18メートルの円墳、横口式石槨 * [[中尾山古墳]] - 対角長約30メートルで三段から五段の八角墳、横口式石槨 * [[キトラ古墳]] - 径20メートルの円墳 * [[束明神古墳]] - 対角長約36メートルの八角墳、凝灰岩製の横口式石槨 * [[マルコ山古墳]] - 径20メートルの円墳、凝灰岩製の横口式石槨 <ref name="kawakami1">[[#河上|河上（2005年）17 - 26ページ]]</ref> ; 飛鳥地域以外の近畿地方 * [[西宮古墳]] - 一辺35メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[峯塚古墳]] - 径35メートルの円墳、横穴式石室 <ref name="kawakami2">[[#河上|河上（2005年）26 - 28ページ]]</ref>
; 関東地方 * [[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]] - 径66メートルの円墳 * [[山室姫塚古墳]] - 径66メートルの円墳 * [[壬生車塚古墳]] - 径62メートルの円墳 * [[総社愛宕山古墳]] - 一辺55メートルの方墳 * [[宝塔山古墳]] - 54×49メートルの方墳 * [[松面古墳]] - 一辺44メートルの方墳 * [[割見塚古墳]] - 一辺40メートルの方墳 * [[蛇穴山古墳]] - 一辺39メートルの方墳 * [[穴八幡古墳]] - 一辺30メートルの方墳 * [[武蔵府中熊野神社古墳]] - 1段目が一辺32メートルの上円下方墳 * [[天文台構内古墳]] - 1段目が一辺28メートルの上円下方墳
; 備後地方 * [[大佐山白塚古墳]] - 一辺12メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[大坊古墳 (福山市)|大坊古墳]]- 墳丘形状不明、花崗岩製の横穴式石室 * [[尾市1号墳|尾市1号古墳]] - 一辺12メートルの八角形、花崗岩製の十字型横口式石槨 * [[猪の子古墳]]- 墳丘形状不明、花崗岩製の横口式石槨
; 北陸地方 * [[河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|河田山12号墳]] - 外護列石、アーチ形切石積横穴式石室を備える方墳
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=広瀬和雄 |year=2010 |title=前方後円墳の終焉|publisher=[[雄山閣]] |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8 |ref=広瀬}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=河上邦彦|authorlink=河上邦彦 |year=2005 |title=大和の終末期古墳|publisher=[[学生社]] |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7 |ref=河上}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=斉藤忠|authorlink=斉藤忠 |year=2004 |title=日本考古学辞典 |publisher=[[三省堂]] |isbn= 978-4-385-15835-8 |ref=斉藤}}{{要検証|date=2016-11-06}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=白石太一郎|authorlink=白石太一郎 |chapter=終末期古墳 |editor1=田中琢|editor2=佐原真|editor2-link=佐原真 |year=2002 |title=日本考古学用語事典 |publisher=学生社 |isbn= 978-4-311-75033-5 |ref=白石}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor1=永原慶二監修|editor1-link=永原慶二|editor2=石上英一他編集|editor2-link=石上英一 |year=1999 |title=岩波 日本史辞典 |publisher=[[岩波書店]] |isbn= 978-4-00-080093-8 |ref=永原}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=河上邦彦 |chapter=終末期古墳 |editor=小野正敏・佐藤信・館野和己 |year=2007 |title=歴史考古学大辞典 |publisher=[[吉川弘文館]] |isbn= 978-4-642-01437-3 |ref=河上}}
== 関連項目 == * [[末期古墳]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]

revision.text:
'''終末期古墳'''（しゅうまつきこふん）は、[[古墳時代]]末から[[飛鳥時代]]にかけての[[7世紀]]頃の[[古墳]]を指す。[[森浩一]]によって最初に提唱された古墳の区分であり<ref>[[森浩一]]編『論集終末期古墳』1972年</ref>、この時代を古墳時代に含むとする考え方と含まないとする考え方がある。終末期古墳としては、[[千葉県]][[栄町]]の[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]や[[山武市]]の[[駄ノ塚古墳]]、[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[キトラ古墳]]や[[高松塚古墳]]などが、特に有名である。
== 概要 == [[6世紀]]末には[[前方後円墳]]築造の時代が終焉を迎えるという大きな変化があり<ref>[[広瀬和雄]]編『前方後円墳の終焉』2010年</ref>、それ以降[[律令制#日本の律令制|律令制]]に向かう段階で造営されたのが終末期古墳である。終末期古墳と認識されるようになるまでは、終末古墳、晩期古墳、飛鳥時代古墳などと呼ばれ、7世紀代の古墳の中でも特殊なものと捉えられていたが、高松塚古墳の調査を契機として終末期古墳として認識され始めた<ref>[[#河上|河上（2005年）6ページ]]</ref><ref>[[#斉藤|斉藤（2004年）]]</ref>。
=== 墳形=== 墳形には、[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]・[[八角墳]]・[[上円下方墳]]などがある。[[天皇陵]]に限っては、前方後円形から大型方墳に変わり、次に八角墳が採用されることになる。そして八角墳には、すべて八角形のものと上八角下方墳の2つのタイプがある。
=== 埋葬施設・副葬品 === 埋葬施設には、[[横穴式石室]]や[[横穴]]のほかに、各種の[[横口式石槨]]、木炭槨などがあり、[[棺]]には[[木棺]]・[[石棺]]以外に[[乾漆棺]]や[[須恵器]]の四注式屋根形[[陶棺]]などがある<ref>[[#永原|永原（1999年）818ページ]]</ref>。副葬品は概して少ない。銅貨銭が特色の一つである。
== 代表的な終末期の古墳 == * 最後の前方後円墳 : [[浅間山古墳 (栄町)|浅間山古墳]] - 墳丘全長78メートルの前方後円墳 : [[胡麻手台16号墳]] - 墳丘全長86メートルの前方後円墳 * 大型方墳 : [[龍角寺岩屋古墳]] - 一辺78メートル、終末期最大の方墳 : [[駄ノ塚古墳]] - 一辺62メートル、西暦[[610年]]から[[620年]]の間に造営された可能性が高い * 天皇陵 : [[春日向山古墳]]（[[用明天皇]]陵）- 63×60メートルの方墳 : [[山田高塚古墳]]（[[推古天皇]]陵）- 63×56メートルの方墳
=== 各地の終末期古墳 === ; 飛鳥地域 * [[赤坂天王山古墳]] - 一辺45メートルの方墳、巨大な横穴式石室 * [[段ノ塚古墳]]（[[舒明天皇|舒明]]陵） - 対角長約45メートルの八角形の墳丘を上に、その下は三段で最下段の幅約90メートル * [[秋殿南古墳]] - 一辺21メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[艸墓古墳]] - 一辺30メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[谷首古墳]] - 一辺35メートルの方墳、巨石の横穴式石室 * [[石舞台古墳]] - 一辺50メートルの周濠・堤、全面に貼石、巨大な横穴式石室 * [[都塚古墳]] - 一辺40メートルの方墳、横穴式石室<ref>{{Cite news |title=都塚古墳は巨大方墳 天皇陵に匹敵 |newspaper=共同通信 |date= |author= |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tLPrEEbKa6A |accessdate=2014-01-08}}</ref> * [[野口王墓|天武・持統陵]] - 対角長約45メートルの八角墳 * [[カナヅカ古墳|平田岩屋古墳]] - 一辺50メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[菖蒲池古墳]] - 方墳、横穴式石室は壁面全体に漆喰塗り、その中に特殊な[[家形石棺]]が2基 * [[岩屋山古墳]] - 一辺45メートルの方形の上に八角形（?）の墳丘、巨大な横穴式石室 * [[牽牛子塚古墳]] - 八角墳、斉明稜の可能性大、凝灰岩をくり抜いた横口式石槨 * [[高松塚古墳]] - 径18メートルの円墳、横口式石槨 * [[中尾山古墳]] - 対角長約30メートルで三段から五段の八角墳、横口式石槨 * [[キトラ古墳]] - 径20メートルの円墳 * [[束明神古墳]] - 対角長約36メートルの八角墳、凝灰岩製の横口式石槨 * [[マルコ山古墳]] - 径20メートルの円墳、凝灰岩製の横口式石槨 <ref name="kawakami1">[[#河上|河上（2005年）17 - 26ページ]]</ref> ; 飛鳥地域以外の近畿地方 * [[西宮古墳]] - 一辺35メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[峯塚古墳]] - 径35メートルの円墳、横穴式石室 <ref name="kawakami2">[[#河上|河上（2005年）26 - 28ページ]]</ref>
; 関東地方 * [[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]] - 径66メートルの円墳 * [[山室姫塚古墳]] - 径66メートルの円墳 * [[壬生車塚古墳]] - 径62メートルの円墳 * [[総社愛宕山古墳]] - 一辺55メートルの方墳 * [[宝塔山古墳]] - 54×49メートルの方墳 * [[松面古墳]] - 一辺44メートルの方墳 * [[割見塚古墳]] - 一辺40メートルの方墳 * [[蛇穴山古墳]] - 一辺39メートルの方墳 * [[穴八幡古墳]] - 一辺30メートルの方墳 * [[武蔵府中熊野神社古墳]] - 1段目が一辺32メートルの上円下方墳 * [[天文台構内古墳]] - 1段目が一辺28メートルの上円下方墳
; 備後地方 * [[大佐山白塚古墳]] - 一辺12メートルの方墳、横穴式石室 * [[大坊古墳 (福山市)|大坊古墳]]- 墳丘形状不明、花崗岩製の横穴式石室 * [[尾市1号墳|尾市1号古墳]] - 一辺12メートルの八角形、花崗岩製の十字型横口式石槨 * [[猪の子古墳]]- 墳丘形状不明、花崗岩製の横口式石槨
; 北陸地方 * [[河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|河田山12号墳]] - 外護列石、アーチ形切石積横穴式石室を備える方墳
== 脚注 == <references/>

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. ; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters

revision.comment: Text replacement - "12=draft" to "qq=draft"
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].

During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.

Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters

; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers

The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers

revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round tumuli|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Round tumuli]] → [[Round Kofun]]
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square tumuli|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Square tumuli]] → [[Square Kofun]]
revision.text:
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Asuka period]], approximately during the [[7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[Chiba Prefecture]], as well as {{ill|Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square Kofun|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * {{ill|Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|qq=}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
