revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11606293]] Wikidata name: final stage kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], approximately during the [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], as well as [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square Kofun|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers

=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-phase kofun|qq=}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11606293]] Wikidata name: final stage kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], approximately during the [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], as well as [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square Kofun|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-phase kofun|qq=}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-phase kofun|qq=}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11606293]] Wikidata name: final stage kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], approximately during the [[:en:7th century|7th century]]. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], as well as [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
During the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square Kofun|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-phase kofun|qq=}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-phase kofun|qq=}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11606293}} [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11606293]] Wikidata name: final stage kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}, approximately during the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, as well as {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
During the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square Kofun|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-phase kofun|qq=}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11606293}} [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: Removing {{afc comment}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]]
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}, approximately during the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, as well as {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
During the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square Kofun|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11606293}} [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Cave tomb" to "Cave Tomb"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]]
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}, approximately during the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, as well as {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
During the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square Kofun|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave Tomb|lt=Cave Tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. * '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}

[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11606293}} [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:終末期古墳]]
'''Shūmatsuki Kofun''' (終末期古墳) refers to kofun (ancient Kofuns) constructed from the end of the [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}, approximately during the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}. This classification of kofun was first proposed by {{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}<ref>{{Cite book |title=Ronsū Shūmatsuki Kofun |editor=Mori Kōichi |year=1972}}</ref>. Some scholars consider this period part of the Kofun period, while others do not. Famous examples include {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, as well as {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
During the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, the construction of [[keyhole-shaped tumuli]] (前方後円墳) came to an end, marking a significant shift<ref>{{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}}</ref>. Shūmatsuki Kofun were built during the transition toward the [[ritsuryō system]] of governance. Before being recognized as Shūmatsuki Kofun, these mounds were referred to as late-period kofun, final-phase kofun, or Asuka-period kofun. The designation gained recognition following the investigation of Takamatsuzuka Kofun<ref>{{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}</ref>.
=== Shapes ===
Typical shapes include: * [[Round Kofun|円墳|1=Round Kofun]] (circular mounds) * [[Square Kofun|方墳|1=Square Kofun]] (square mounds) * [[Octagonal tumuli|八角墳]] (octagonal mounds) * [[Ste11ed tumuli|上円下方墳]] (ste11ed circular mounds on square bases)
For imperial mausoleums, the shape transitioned from keyhole-shaped tumuli to large square mounds, and eventually to octagonal mounds. Two types of octagonal mounds are known: fully octagonal and octagonal structures atop a square base.
=== Burial Chambers and Grave Goods ===
Burial chambers varied, including: * {{ill|Corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}} * Various types of {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} * Charcoal chambers
Coffins included [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]], {{ill|sekkan|lt=stone sarcophagi|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|lacquered coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}, and {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} shaped like houses, made from {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. Grave goods were generally sparse, but the presence of bronze coins is a distinctive feature<ref>{{Cite book |editor=Nagahara Keiji |year=1999 |title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |isbn=978-4-00-080093-8}}</ref>.
== Notable Shūmatsuki Kofun ==
* '''Last Keyhole-shaped Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|ja|浅間山古墳 (栄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengenyama Kofun (Sakae)|qq=}} - Total length: 78 meters ''' {{ill|Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|ja|胡麻手台16号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomate-dai 16-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Total length: 86 meters * '''Large Square Tumuli''' ''' {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūkakuji-Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} - Side length: 78 meters, the largest Shūmatsuki square tumulus ''' {{ill|Danotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Side length: 62 meters; likely built between 610 and 620 AD * '''Imperial Mausoleums''' ''' {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 60 meters ''' {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko’s Mausoleum) - 63 × 56 meters
=== Regional Examples ===
; '''Asuka Region''' * [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] - Square tumulus, side length: 45 meters * {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} - Octagonal tumulus, diagonal: 45 meters * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} - Diameter: 20 meters
; '''Kinki Region (outside Asuka)''' * {{ill|Nishinomiya Kofun|ja|西宮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 35 meters
; '''Kanto Region''' * {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyoda)|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Gyōda)|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters * {{ill|Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun|lt=Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|ja|山室姫塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro-Himezuka Kofun|qq=}} - Diameter: 66 meters
; '''Chugoku Region''' * {{ill|Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|1=Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaya Shirotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus, side length: 12 meters
; '''Hokuriku Region''' * {{ill|Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|ja|河田山古墳群#河田山12号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawadayama 12-gō Kofun|qq=}} - Square tumulus with arched stone chambers
== References == <references/>
== Related Literature == * {{Cite book |author=Hirose Kazuo |year=2010 |title=Zenchōkōenfun no Shūen |publisher=Yūzankaku |isbn=978-4-639-02156-8}} * {{Cite book |author=Kawakami Kunihiko |year=2005 |title=Yamato no Shūmatsuki Kofun |publisher=Gakuseisha |isbn=978-4-311-20282-7}} * {{Cite book |author=Saitō Tadashi |year=2004 |title=Japanese Archaeology Dictionary |publisher=Sanseido |isbn=978-4-385-15835-8}}
== See Also == * {{ill|terminal-phase kofun|ja|末期古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal-phase kofun|qq=}} * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
